result:
Compile is clean. Per instructions I won't re-run the program.

**Root cause:** `CallInfo::is_lua()` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:309` returned `matches!(self.u, CallInfoFrame::Lua { .. })`, but in C-Lua the canonical truth is `!(ci->callstatus & CIST_C)`. `prep_call_info` (`crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:743`) only writes `callstatus`, not the `u` variant, so the `u` field was stale from whatever previously inhabited that CallInfo slot. When a C call (e.g. `pcall`→`f_close`) reused a slot last used by a Lua frame, `is_lua()` returned `true` even though the callstatus said `CIST_C`, and `debug.rs:1816 ci_lua_proto` panicked because the slot held a C closure.

**Fix:**
1. `CallInfo::is_lua()` now checks `(self.callstatus & CIST_C) == 0`, matching C semantics.
2. `prep_call_info` now also resets `ci.u` to `c_default()` or `lua_default()` based on the mask, so `saved_pc()`/`nextra_args()`/etc. observe a consistent payload.

Temporary `eprintln!` removed. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Did not re-test per instructions; the mega-loop will re-run.
